name: SM-HW-S1b-004_DriveRoller_v01
_comment: S1b DriveRoller per SW_Design_Guide.md §13.2. Roller body built via boss_extrude_blind (circle on Front Plane, axis=+Z), NOT revolve_boss, so face sketches on the end faces work normally. Z=0 = one bearing-end face (part origin at center of this face); Z=80mm = other end. Bearing pockets 7mm deep from each end face. O-ring groove (5mm wide x 1mm deep) at Z=40 mid-length, sketched on Top Plane (sketch_x=part_X, sketch_y=part_Z), centerline along sketch_y at sketch_x=0, profile at sketch_x=+12.0 (positive-X side -- no opposite-side handedness constraint since body is extruded not revolved). Profile top edge at sketch_x=12.5 = body outer surface; bottom at 11.5 = 1mm into body.

features._comment: O-ring groove: 5mm wide along part-Z, 1mm deep into part-X, at Z=40 (mid-length). Sketched on Top Plane (XZ). Convention: center.x/y/z are part-frame; width extends along the plane's first in-plane axis (Top Plane => part-X), height along the second (Top Plane => part-Z). So width=1 carves 1mm radially (part-X spans [11.5, 12.5], just under the cylinder surface at R=12.5) and height=5 spans 5mm along the cylinder axis (part-Z spans [37.5, 42.5], straddling Z=40). The centerline IS the cylinder's central Z axis (part_X=0, part_Y=0), running Z=-5 to Z=85 so the rect profile can revolve fully without crossing it.
features.name: SK_BeltGripGroove
